title: Better uncertainty calibration for LLM classifiers
summary: This paper introduces Thermodynamic Entropy Calibration (TEC), a physics-inspired post-hoc calibration method for LLM classifiers that adjusts per-sample temperature based on predictive entropy and decision margin. The paper has been compiled to a 14-page PDF with all required sections: abstract, introduction, related work, methods, experiments, discussion, conclusion, acknowledgments, and appendix. All 4 figures from <available_figures> are included in the paper at their designated locations (fig1: TEC method overview; fig2: ECE comparison across 5 datasets; fig3: ECE decomposition by easy/hard splits; fig4: reliability diagrams). The bibliography (references.bib) contains 18 entries covering calibration methods, entropy-based uncertainty estimation, thermodynamic principles, and benchmarks. The paper was compiled using pdflatex and bibtex with no critical errors. The PDF is ready for submission to a top-tier conference (14 pages, letter paper size, 1.6MB).
